seo.title: S. Dakota Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in South Dakota — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license south dakota, dental assistant certification south dakota, RDA south dakota, DANB CDA south dakota, dental assistant radiography south dakota

board.name: South Dakota State Board of Dentistry

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in South Dakota.

steps.title: Create an Online Account
steps.description: Visit the South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal and click 'Register Here' for first-time users. Complete the registration process by providing the required personal and account information.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal and initiate the application for Expanded Function Dental Assistant certification. Fill out all sections accurately.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents through the South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal, including official transcripts from Board-approved courses (e.g., restorative, orthodontic), current CPR certification, and any other specified proofs of qualification.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided during the application process to complete and submit fingerprints for both state and federal criminal background checks.

steps.title: Pay Application and Certification Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee through the South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal. Upon approval of your application, an initial certification fee will also be assessed and must be paid.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: South Dakota does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, South Dakota does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in South Dakota.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in South Dakota?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in South Dakota. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: South Dakota does not license general dental assistants.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant certification in South Dakota, use the South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal.

official_links.portal_name: South Dakota Board of Dentistry Online Application Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal and click 'Register Here' under 'First Time Users'. Provide your name, email, desired username, password, and security question to create your account.
